prompt: What does `(3,8)` mean in Strudel? I saw it in a pattern like `s("bd(3,8)")`

assertions.description: Explains the beats and segments parameters
assertions.check: Response explains beats and segments/steps parameters of Euclidean notation

assertions.description: Mentions Euclidean rhythm or distributing-evenly concept
assertions.check: Response mentions Euclidean rhythm or the distributing-evenly concept

assertions.description: Mentions the optional third offset parameter
assertions.check: Response mentions the optional third offset parameter (e.g., (3,8,2))

assertions.description: Provides a code example with Euclidean notation
assertions.check: Response provides at least one code example with Euclidean notation
